items.title: BERT feed-forward block
items.scenario: In BERT-base, each Transformer layer has a two-layer MLP: Linear → GELU → Linear. Hidden states of shape (batch, seq_len, 768) pass through GELU element-wise before the second projection expands to 3072 and back.
items.explanation: GELU is the non-linearity between the up-projection and down-projection in the FFN sublayer—this pattern is repeated for every layer and head stack in the encoder.

items.title: Soft negative handling in embeddings
items.scenario: A token embedding dimension receives a slightly negative pre-activation (-0.5) after layer normalization. ReLU would zero it out; GELU returns about -0.15, preserving a small gradient path.
items.explanation: The graded response near zero lets the network transmit weak negative evidence instead of a hard cutoff, which can matter in deep stacks with residual connections.

items.title: Matching a pretrained checkpoint
items.scenario: You fine-tune `bert-base-uncased` in PyTorch but swap `nn.GELU()` for `nn.ReLU()` in a custom head. Loss diverges because the backbone expects GELU statistics and gradient flow from pretraining.
items.explanation: Pretrained Transformer weights assume the original activation; changing GELU to ReLU in the FFN breaks the distribution the weights were trained under.
